subject: Ecology
title: Salinization
description: A quick, practical guide to Salinization.

hook.text: Irrigation saved farmland from drought—then slowly poisoned it with salt.

buildup.text: Water evaporates from irrigated fields, leaving dissolved salts behind in the soil.

discovery.text: Over time, salt builds up until crops can no longer extract water from the soil.

twist.text: The ancient Mesopotamians irrigated their fields for centuries—then collapsed as soils salted up.

climax.text: Today, salinization degrades about 2,000 hectares of farmland every day worldwide.

punchline.text: The water that grows crops can also kill them.

quiz.question: What causes soil salinization in irrigated fields?
options: Too much rainfall dilutes the soil
